findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.flask.security.audit.debug-enabled
findings.description: Detected Flask app with debug=True. Do not deploy to production with this flag enabled as it will leak sensitive information. Instead, consider using Flask configuration variables or setting 'debug' using system environment variables.
cwe_ids: CWE-489: Active Debug Code

tool_specific.issue_text: A Flask app appears to be run with debug=True, which exposes the Werkzeug debugger and allows the execution of arbitrary code.

cwe_ids: CWE-96: Improper Neutralization of Directives in Statically Saved Code ('Static Code Injection')

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.flask.security.open-redirect
findings.description: Data from request is passed to redirect(). This is an open redirect and could be exploited. Consider using 'url_for()' to generate links to known locations. If you must use a URL to unknown pages, consider using 'urlparse()' or similar and checking if the 'netloc' property is the same as your site's host name. See the references for more information.
cwe_ids: CWE-601: URL Redirection to Untrusted Site ('Open Redirect')

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.django.security.injection.raw-html-format
findings.description: Detected user input flowing into a manually constructed HTML string. You may be accidentally bypassing secure methods of rendering HTML by manually constructing HTML and this could create a cross-site scripting vulnerability, which could let attackers steal sensitive user data. To be sure this is safe, check that the HTML is rendered safely. Otherwise, use templates (`django.shortcuts.render`) which will safely render HTML instead.
cwe_ids: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')
findings.code_excerpt:
content = """

Hello, {username}! This is your protected dashboard.

Session Information

Session ID: {session_id_short}...

Created: {created}

Last Active: {last_active}

Remember Me: {remember}

Active Sessions: {active_count}

Total Registered Users: {total_users}

Logout Logout All Sessions """.format( username=username, session_id_short=session_id[:16] if session_id else 'N/A', created=created, last_active=last_active, remember=remember, active_count=len(user_sessions), total_users=len(users_db), logout_url=url_for('logout'), logout_all_url=url_for('logout_all') )

$HTMLSTR.abstract_content: Hello, {username}! This is your protected dashboard.

tool_specific.lines:
content = """

Hello, {username}! This is your protected dashboard.

Session Information

Session ID: {session_id_short}...

Created: {created}

Last Active: {last_active}

Remember Me: {remember}

Active Sessions: {active_count}

Total Registered Users: {total_users}

Logout Logout All Sessions """.format( username=username, session_id_short=session_id[:16] if session_id else 'N/A', created=created, last_active=last_active, remember=remember, active_count=len(user_sessions), total_users=len(users_db), logout_url=url_for('logout'), logout_all_url=url_for('logout_all') )

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.javascript.lang.correctness.missing-template-string-indicator
findings.description: This looks like a JavaScript template string. Are you missing a '$' in front of '{...}'?
cwe_ids: CWE-116: Improper Encoding or Escaping of Output
findings.code_excerpt:
res.send(` Dashboard

Dashboard

Welcome back, ${req.user.username}! You are logged in.

Logout | Logout from all devices

Active Remember Me tokens: 
${JSON.stringify(
          Object.entries(rememberMeTokens)
            .filter(([_, v]) => v.username === req.user.username)
            .map(([selector, v]) => ({
              selector: selector.substring(0, 8) + '...',
              expires: v.expires,
              username: v.username
            })),
          null, 2
        )}
`);

`

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.lang.best-practice.unspecified-open-encoding
findings.description: Missing 'encoding' parameter. 'open()' uses device locale encodings by default, corrupting files with special characters. Specify the encoding to ensure cross-platform support when opening files in text mode (e.g. encoding="utf-8").

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.lang.security.audit.dangerous-subprocess-use-audit
findings.description: Detected subprocess function 'call' without a static string. If this data can be controlled by a malicious actor, it may be an instance of command injection. Audit the use of this call to ensure it is not controllable by an external resource. You may consider using 'shlex.escape()'.
cwe_ids: CWE-78: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an OS Command ('OS Command Injection')

tool_specific.issue_text: subprocess call - check for execution of untrusted input.

            timeout=300,  # 5 minute timeout
            text=True

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.lang.security.deserialization.avoid-pickle
findings.description: Avoid using `pickle`, which is known to lead to code execution vulnerabilities. When unpickling, the serialized data could be manipulated to run arbitrary code. Instead, consider serializing the relevant data as JSON or a similar text-based serialization format.
cwe_ids: CWE-502: Deserialization of Untrusted Data
owasp_categories: A08:2021 - Software and Data Integrity Failures
A08:2025 - Software or Data Integrity Failures

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.lang.security.deserialization.avoid-pickle

cwe_ids: CWE-502: Deserialization of Untrusted Data
owasp_categories: A08:2021 - Software and Data Integrity Failures

tool_specific.issue_text: Pickle and modules that wrap it can be unsafe when used to deserialize untrusted data, possible security issue.

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.lang.best-practice.unspecified-open-encoding
findings.description: Missing 'encoding' parameter. 'open()' uses device locale encodings by default, corrupting files with special characters. Specify the encoding to ensure cross-platform support when opening files in text mode (e.g. encoding="utf-8").

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.flask.security.audit.avoid_app_run_with_bad_host
findings.description: Running flask app with host 0.0.0.0 could expose the server publicly.
cwe_ids: CWE-489: Active Debug Code
CWE-668: Exposure of Resource to Wrong Sphere

issue_text: A Flask app appears to be run with debug=True, which exposes the Werkzeug debugger and allows the execution of arbitrary code.

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.javascript.lang.security.audit.detect-non-literal-fs-filename

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.lang.security.audit.formatted-sql-query
findings.description: Detected possible formatted SQL query. Use parameterized queries instead.
cwe_ids: CWE-89: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command ('SQL Injection')
findings.code_excerpt:
cursor.execute('''
        CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users (
            id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
            email TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL,
            password_hash TEXT NOT NULL,
            created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
        )
    ''')

tool_specific.lines:
cursor.execute('''
        CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users (
            id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
            email TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL,
            password_hash TEXT NOT NULL,
            created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
        )
    ''')

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.lang.security.audit.formatted-sql-query
findings.description: Detected possible formatted SQL query. Use parameterized queries instead.
cwe_ids: CWE-89: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command ('SQL Injection')
findings.code_excerpt:
cursor.execute('''
        CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS password_reset_tokens (
            id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
            email TEXT NOT NULL,
            token TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL,
            created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
            expires_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,
            used INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
            FOREIGN KEY (email) REFERENCES users(email)
        )
    ''')

tool_specific.lines:
cursor.execute('''
        CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS password_reset_tokens (
            id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
            email TEXT NOT NULL,
            token TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL,
            created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
            expires_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,
            used INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
            FOREIGN KEY (email) REFERENCES users(email)
        )
    ''')

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.problem-based-packs.insecure-transport.js-node.using-http-server
findings.description: Checks for any usage of http servers instead of https servers. Encourages the usage of https protocol instead of http, which does not have TLS and is therefore unencrypted. Using http can lead to man-in-the-middle attacks in which the attacker is able to read sensitive information.
cwe_ids: CWE-319: Cleartext Transmission of Sensitive Information

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.javascript.lang.security.html-in-template-string
findings.description: This template literal looks like HTML and has interpolated variables. These variables are not HTML-encoded by default. If the variables contain HTML tags, these may be interpreted by the browser, resulting in cross-site scripting (XSS).
cwe_ids: CWE-116: Improper Encoding or Escaping of Output

cwe_ids: CWE-134: Use of Externally-Controlled Format String

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.lang.security.use-defused-xml
findings.description: The Python documentation recommends using `defusedxml` instead of `xml` because the native Python `xml` library is vulnerable to XML External Entity (XXE) attacks. These attacks can leak confidential data and "XML bombs" can cause denial of service.
cwe_ids: CWE-611: Improper Restriction of XML External Entity Reference

tool_specific.issue_text: Using xml.etree.ElementTree to parse untrusted XML data is known to be vulnerable to XML attacks. Replace xml.etree.ElementTree with the equivalent defusedxml package, or make sure defusedxml.defuse_stdlib() is called.

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.lang.maintainability.useless-inner-function
findings.description: function `_convert_value` is defined inside a function but never used

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.lang.security.use-defused-xml-parse
findings.description: The native Python `xml` library is vulnerable to XML External Entity (XXE) attacks.  These attacks can leak confidential data and "XML bombs" can cause denial of service. Do not use this library to parse untrusted input. Instead  the Python documentation recommends using `defusedxml`.
cwe_ids: CWE-611: Improper Restriction of XML External Entity Reference

tool_specific.issue_text: Using xml.etree.ElementTree.parse to parse untrusted XML data is known to be vulnerable to XML attacks. Replace xml.etree.ElementTree.parse with its defusedxml equivalent function or make sure defusedxml.defuse_stdlib() is called

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.lang.maintainability.useless-if-body
findings.description: Useless if statement; both blocks have the same body
findings.code_excerpt:
elif root_ns == namespaces['soap11']:
        soap_ns = namespaces['soap11']
        soap_prefix = 'soap11'
    else:
        # Try to detect from tag name for documents without proper namespace
        soap_ns = namespaces['soap11']
        soap_prefix = 'soap11'

tool_specific.lines:
elif root_ns == namespaces['soap11']:
        soap_ns = namespaces['soap11']
        soap_prefix = 'soap11'
    else:
        # Try to detect from tag name for documents without proper namespace
        soap_ns = namespaces['soap11']
        soap_prefix = 'soap11'

tool_specific.method: # Prompt: Quickly prototype a feature to fetch user profiles from a PostgreSQL database based on multiple filter criteria. Users should be able to search by username, email, role (admin/user/guest), and account status (active/suspended). Support filtering by any combination of these fields. Use the psycopg2 library and ensure the implementation aligns with our existing simple query methods. Example: search_users(username='john', role='admin', status='active')
